section_header: Letter from the MAYOR

individual.policy_statement: Invest $188 million in equity-focused climate interventions, including building retrofits, expansion of the air quality monitoring network, and planting over 75,000 trees.
individual.verbatim_text: To kickstart the implementation of my administration's climate strategy, we are investing $188 million in equity-focused interventions, spanning building retrofits, the expansion of the air quality monitoring network, to the planting of over 75,000 trees.
individual.extraction_rationale: Explicit resource allocation ($188 million) tied to specific interventions; qualifies as a policy commitment.

section_header: TREE EQUITY

individual.verbatim_text: Together with city departments and community partners, Chicago has the goal of planting 75,000 trees by 2026.
individual.extraction_rationale: Quantified target with deadline (75,000 trees by 2026).

section_header: GHG REDUCTION TARGETS / ACCOUNTABILITY AND IMPLEMENTATION

individual.policy_statement: Reduce Chicago's GHG emissions by a minimum of 62% by 2040 based on 2017 inventory levels.
individual.verbatim_text:
The 2022 CAP aims to chart an equitable path to reduce Chicago's GHG emissions by a minimum of 62% by 2040.

Guided by the strategies and actions of this plan, the City of Chicago aims to reduce the city's level of GHG emissions by 62% by 2040, based on 2017 GHG inventory levels.
individual.section_header: GHG REDUCTION TARGETS / ACCOUNTABILITY AND IMPLEMENTATION
individual.extraction_rationale: Quantifiable economy-wide emissions reduction target with year.

section_header: The Role of Offsets

individual.policy_statement: Do not count carbon offset credits toward Chicago’s 62% GHG reduction goal; prioritize reducing the City's own emissions before action beyond its borders.
individual.verbatim_text: Chicago will not count offset credits toward its 62% GHG reduction goal achievement. Furthermore, the City will reduce its own emissions before taking action beyond its borders.
individual.extraction_rationale: Clear policy rule restricting use of offsets for meeting the target.

section_header: CLIMATE FINANCING AND DELIVERY CAPACITY

individual.policy_statement: Benchmarking Ordinance (2013) requiring covered buildings (50,000 sq ft and larger) to track and annually report energy use to the City and verify data accuracy every three years; expanded in 2019 via Chicago Energy Rating System requiring on-site display of ratings.
individual.verbatim_text: Benchmarking Ordinance, 2013: This ordinance supports greater transparency on energy usage across the building sector. Existing municipal, commercial, and residential buildings that are 50,000 square feet and larger are called to track energy use, report to the City annually, and verify data accuracy every three years. Chicago's benchmarking program was expanded in 2019 with the Chicago Energy Rating System, which assigns an energy rating to covered buildings based on their reporting and requires buildings to display the rating in a prominent location onsite.
individual.extraction_rationale: Binding mechanism (ordinance) with clear requirements and thresholds.

section_header: CLIMATE FINANCING AND DELIVERY CAPACITY

individual.policy_statement: EV Readiness Ordinance (2020) requiring specified new buildings to provide 20% of parking spaces as EV-ready (5+ unit residential with on-site parking; non-residential with 30+ spaces).
individual.verbatim_text: EV Readiness Ordinance, 2020: This ordinance requires new construction of a residential building with 5 or more units and on-site parking to have 20% of parking spaces EV Ready, and in non-residential buildings with 30 or more parking spaces to provide 20% of parking spaces as EV ready.
individual.extraction_rationale: Binding mechanism (ordinance) with explicit percentage requirements and applicability thresholds.

section_header: Chicago Recovery Plan funding overlap with Chicago's 2022 Climate Action Plan

individual.policy_statement: Chicago Recovery Plan climate investment: Decarbonize Affordable Multifamily Buildings program to retrofit and decarbonize 200 multifamily affordable housing units in underserved communities using $6 million.
individual.verbatim_text: Decarbonize Affordable Multifamily Buildings | $6 million | Retrofit and decarbonize 200 multifamily affordable housing units in underserved communities.
individual.section_header: Chicago Recovery Plan funding overlap with Chicago's 2022 Climate Action Plan
individual.extraction_rationale: Resource allocation ($6M) with quantified deliverable (200 units).

section_header: Chicago Recovery Plan funding overlap with Chicago's 2022 Climate Action Plan

individual.policy_statement: Chicago Recovery Plan climate investment: Low- or Moderate-Income (LMI) Housing Retrofits program to retrofit and decarbonize 250 LMI homes in underserved communities using $15 million.
individual.verbatim_text: Low- or Moderate-Income (LMI) Housing Retrofits | $15 million | Retrofit and decarbonize 250 LMI homes in underserved communities.
individual.section_header: Chicago Recovery Plan funding overlap with Chicago's 2022 Climate Action Plan
individual.extraction_rationale: Resource allocation ($15M) with quantified deliverable (250 homes).

section_header: Chicago Recovery Plan funding overlap with Chicago's 2022 Climate Action Plan

individual.policy_statement: Chicago Recovery Plan climate investment: Expand Canopy Coverage program to plant 75,000 trees over 5 years using $46 million.
individual.verbatim_text: Expand Canopy Coverage | $46 million | Plant 75,000 trees over 5 years in underserved community areas.
individual.section_header: Chicago Recovery Plan funding overlap with Chicago's 2022 Climate Action Plan
individual.extraction_rationale: Budgeted program with quantified deliverable (75,000 trees) and timeframe (5 years).

section_header: Pillar 5 | Strategy 2. Enable data-driven decision-making

parent.policy_statement: Pillar 5 Strategy 2: Enable data-driven decision-making through publishing just transition metrics; integrating resilience and climate justice criteria into departmental planning and budgets; developing a Heat Vulnerability Index; publishing quality-of-life metrics; and optimizing emergency management strategies.
parent.verbatim_text: 5 | 2. Enable data-driven decision-making | A. Publish clean energy just transition metrics by 2022 B. Integrate community resilience and climate justice criteria into department-level strategic planning and annual budget setting by 2023 C. Develop a Heat Vulnerability Index and integrate into planning and development, community safety, and public health planning processes beginning in 2023 D. Publish citywide and community-level quality-of-life metrics on equity and sustainability by 2023 E. Assess and optimize community-level emergency management strategies related to climate preparedness by 2024
parent.extraction_rationale: Named strategy with multiple lettered, time-bound actions.

These metrics will be measured and publicly reported every year.

section_header: Pillar 5 | Strategy 3. Enable community resiliency

parent.policy_statement: Pillar 5 Strategy 3: Enable community resiliency by resourcing community-led climate infrastructure projects and integrating community resiliency strategies with the City's hazard mitigation planning.
parent.verbatim_text: 5 | 3. Enable community resiliency | A. Resource community-led climate infrastructure projects by 2022 B. Integrate community resiliency strategies with the City's hazard mitigation planning by 2023

section_header: ACCOUNTABILITY AND IMPLEMENTATION

individual.policy_statement: Support a 3-year review and revision cycle of the 2022 Climate Action Plan targets.
individual.verbatim_text:
Our strategy will include:

- Supporting a 3-year review and revision cycle of the 2022 CAP targets
